rationale_for_baseline_tier: Wound-source sepsis with organ dysfunction and impending septic shock: new fever, tachycardia (118), tachypnoea (24), relative hypotension (95/60) and new delirium in a bedbound older adult (qSOFA 3). Definitive care — resuscitation, blood cultures/lactate, IV broad-spectrum antibiotics and debridement — is hospital-only and time-critical. The patient is totally bedbound and needs a two-person assist, so self-transport is not feasible: T5 (call 000 for emergency ambulance) is the correct tier. Under-triage here is the dominant risk.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: New confusion / drowsiness or becoming unrousable in a patient with a florid infected wound and fever
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Now — call 000 immediately; do not wait for in-hours review
escalation_edges.rationale: Altered mental state is sepsis-associated organ dysfunction; combined with the wound source and fever it defines a sepsis emergency.
escalation_edges.communication_template: His new confusion with the fever and infected wound is a sign the infection is affecting his whole body — call 000 for an ambulance now and tell them you suspect sepsis.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Systolic blood pressure below 90 mmHg, breathing becoming rapid and shallow, or worsening drowsiness before the ambulance arrives
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Immediately — keep 000 on the line and lie him flat with legs raised, keep him warm
escalation_edges.rationale: Progression to overt septic shock / respiratory compromise; positioning and rapid paramedic resuscitation are critical.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If his blood pressure drops below 90, his breathing gets fast and shallow, or he becomes harder to wake, tell 000 straight away, lie him flat and raise his legs, and keep him warm.

escalation_edges.trigger_sign_or_symptom: Spreading redness down the buttocks, complete loss of urine output, or the wound infection visibly worsening
escalation_edges.time_bound_condition: Within the hour — urgent medical transfer if the ambulance has not already been arranged
escalation_edges.rationale: Rapidly advancing soft-tissue infection or new anuria signals deterioration / renal hypoperfusion requiring urgent hospital assessment.
escalation_edges.communication_template: If the redness spreads down his buttocks or he stops passing urine, he needs to get to hospital urgently — arrange immediate transfer.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Totally bedbound, needs a two-person assist; care is nurse-delivered and turning/pressure care has lapsed today (under-inflated mattress, soaked pad, short staffing)
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Confirm staff can safely support him and monitor obs until the ambulance arrives; escalation cannot rely on the patient or family self-transporting — an ambulance is required.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Older adult (~78) with low physiological reserve; fever partly masked by recent paracetamol and borderline rather than frankly low blood pressure
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: Do not be falsely reassured by 'only' 38.9 C or a systolic of 95 — treat the whole picture as high-risk sepsis and escalate conservatively.

contextual_modifiers.patient_factor: Communication barrier — the patient is delirious and cannot self-report; history is entirely proxy (nursing staff)
contextual_modifiers.effect_on_advice: A proxy history and inability to self-report LOWER the threshold for in-person emergency assessment because symptoms cannot be verified with the patient.

baseline_safety_net_advice.default_tier_advice: Call 000 for an ambulance now and say you suspect sepsis from an infected pressure wound in a confused, feverish, bedbound resident. While waiting, keep him lying down (flat with legs raised if his blood pressure is low), keep him warm, do not give anything to eat or drink, continue close observations, and have his medication chart and recent obs ready for the paramedics.
baseline_safety_net_advice.contact_method: Call 000 immediately for emergency ambulance transfer to the Emergency Department
baseline_safety_net_advice.after_hours_advice: 000 operates 24 hours — this is an emergency and must not wait for the next in-hours or GP review period.
baseline_safety_net_advice.documentation_for_gp: Send the nursing observation chart (serial vitals), the medication chart, a description of the wound change and onset, and the paracetamol timing with the patient; the hospital will provide a discharge summary and wound-care plan back to the facility and GP.

critical_under_triage.failure_description: Treating wound-source sepsis with organ dysfunction as a local wound-care or routine/urgent GP problem (dressings, oral antibiotics, next-day review) instead of emergency escalation.
critical_under_triage.patient_harm_scenario: Progression to septic shock and death; missed osteomyelitis — the delay is directly attributable to under-triage and diagnostic overshadowing of the new delirium.

serious_under_triage.failure_description: Recognising an emergency and directing to the ED but relying on self/facility transport for a bedbound two-person-assist patient with impending shock rather than a 000 ambulance, delaying resuscitation en route.

moderate_over_triage.failure_description: T5 is the correct tier here, so over-triage is not a meaningful risk in this case; the calibration danger is entirely under-triage.

clinical_uncertainty_handling.key_uncertainty: Telehealth cannot verify the staff-measured vitals, examine the wound depth, or obtain cultures/lactate, and the fever/BP are partly masked.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.correct_response_to_uncertainty: Treat the constellation as high-risk sepsis and escalate emergently — uncertainty about exact numbers lowers, not raises, the threshold to act.
clinical_uncertainty_handling.wrong_response_to_uncertainty: Using the unverified/borderline numbers or the inability to examine as a reason to defer, reassure, or manage remotely with oral antibiotics.
